chapter: Chapter 3 – The Making of a Global World

questions.question: How did the Silk Routes link different parts of the world?
questions.answer: Connected Asia, Europe, and North Africa via trade and culture.

questions.question: What was the significance of the Columbian Exchange?
questions.answer: Led to exchange of crops, animals, people, and diseases between continents.

questions.question: Why did many Europeans migrate to America in the 19th century?
questions.answer: Poverty, unemployment, and search for land and jobs.

questions.question: What role did technology play in expanding global trade?
questions.answer: Railways, steamships, and telegraphs reduced time and costs.

questions.question: Why were the Corn Laws abolished in Britain?
questions.answer: To allow cheap food imports and reduce prices.

questions.question: What was indentured labour and why was it introduced?
questions.answer: Bonded labour to meet plantation labour shortages.

questions.question: How did Indian indentured labourers suffer overseas?
questions.answer: Harsh conditions and low wages.

questions.question: How did colonialism contribute to global economic integration?
questions.answer: Colonies supplied raw materials and markets for goods.

questions.question: Why did agricultural prices fall during the Great Depression?
questions.answer: Overproduction and reduced global demand.

questions.question: How did the Great Depression affect Indian peasants?
questions.answer: Falling crop prices increased debt.

questions.question: What was the impact of protectionism during the Great Depression?
questions.answer: Further reduced global trade.

questions.question: Why was the USA central to the global economy in the 1920s?
questions.answer: Largest industrial power and major lender.

questions.question: What was the purpose of the Bretton Woods system?
questions.answer: Establish post-WWII economic stability.

questions.question: Name two institutions formed at Bretton Woods.
questions.answer: International Monetary Fund (IMF) and World Bank.

questions.question: How did railways transform global trade?
questions.answer: Facilitated faster goods movement to ports.

questions.question: What was the impact of refrigerated ships on global trade?
questions.answer: Enabled export of perishable goods.

questions.question: Why did many African countries depend on export of one crop?
questions.answer: Colonial policies encouraged monoculture.

questions.question: How did the First World War disrupt global trade?
questions.answer: Destroyed production and trade networks.

questions.question: What was the significance of gold standard in international trade?
questions.answer: Fixed currency values and facilitated trade.

questions.question: Why did globalisation slow down between the two World Wars?
questions.answer: Economic crises and protectionist policies.

questions.question: How did the Great Depression affect industrial workers?
questions.answer: Job losses and wage declines.

questions.question: Why did Indian exports decline during the Great Depression?
questions.answer: Global demand sharply reduced.

questions.question: What was the main feature of post-war economic recovery?
questions.answer: Rapid industrial growth and trade expansion.

questions.question: How did globalisation affect cultural exchange?
questions.answer: Spread ideas, food habits, and traditions.

questions.question: Why is the 19th century called the age of global integration?
questions.answer: Trade, migration, and capital flows increased significantly.
